title: euonymus wilsonii
intro: Euonymus wilsonii, also known as the Wilson's spindletree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. This deciduous shrub or small tree is native to China and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and have been reported to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Euonymus wilsonii include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and other remedies.
